prerequisites: A Google account whose Gmail you want to read, or a Google Workspace account for service-account access.
A Google Cloud project with the Gmail API enabled.
OAuth or service-account credentials to populate the required credentials config field. The optional start_date, include_spam_and_trash, and num_workers fields can be left at their defaults.
Read access via the Gmail API read-only scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as a Gmail user with OAuth 2.0. Maps to the secret fields credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.client_refresh_token. Create an OAuth client ID in the Google Cloud Console, complete the consent flow to obtain a refresh token, and supply the client ID, client secret, and refresh token.

authMethods.name: Service account
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a Google service account. Maps to the secret field credentials.service_account_info, which holds the service account key JSON. To read a Google Workspace user's mailbox, the service account must be granted domain-wide delegation for the gmail.readonly scope by a Workspace super administrator.

setupSteps.title: Create a Google Cloud project and enable the Gmail API
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console, create or select a project and enable the Gmail API for it. The Gmail API is the REST interface this source reads from.

setupSteps.title: Choose OAuth or a service account
setupSteps.body: Use OAuth 2.0 to read a single user's mailbox after they grant consent, or use a service account (with domain-wide delegation) to read mailboxes across a Google Workspace domain. Both paths request the read-only scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly.

setupSteps.title: Create OAuth credentials (OAuth path)
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console, create an OAuth client ID and configure the OAuth consent screen. Complete the OAuth flow to obtain a refresh token, then provide the client ID, client secret, and refresh token for the credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.client_refresh_token fields.

setupSteps.title: Create a service account and key (service-account path)
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud Console under IAM and Admin > Service Accounts, create a service account, add a key, and download the key as JSON. Paste the key JSON into credentials.service_account_info. For Google Workspace, a super administrator must add the service account's client ID under Admin console > Security > API controls > Manage Domain Wide Delegation and authorize the gmail.readonly scope.

setupSteps.title: Configure the optional source fields
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to limit the earliest records to sync. Enable include_spam_and_trash to also read messages in Spam and Trash. Set num_workers to control parallelism. Leave any of these unset to use their defaults; only the credentials field is required.

sources.title: Using OAuth 2.0 to Access Google APIs
Create access credentials (OAuth client ID and service account)
